evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG/DMI requester
evidence_slots.object: hart debug controls and CPU execution control
evidence_slots.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI requests that can halt/resume harts or enter debug mode should be mediated by debug authorization/authentication such as jtag_key match, debug enable/lifecycle state, or equivalent access control before asserting hart debug controls.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag accepts TAP DMIACCESS traffic and forwards dmi_req through dmi_cdc to dm_csrs. dm_csrs sets dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1 with comment 'no authentication implemented'. DMControl writes assign dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data; haltreq/resumereq are derived from dmcontrol_q and connected through dm_top into dm_mem/debug_req_o path. jtag_key is only copied to pass on reset in the observed dmi_jtag excerpt; no comparison gate was observed in the request-forwarding or DMControl write path.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins/tap -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> dmi_jtag state/request -> dmi_cdc -> dm_csrs DMControl -> dm_top dm_mem -> debug_req_o/haltreq/resumereq
evidence_slots.state_condition: trst_ni resets dmi_jtag shift/request state; dmi_rst_no is assigned rst_ni; dm_csrs clears most debug state when dmcontrol_q.dmactive is low but allows dmactive to be written. No authenticated state condition beyond unconditional dmstatus.authenticated=1 was observed.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated external JTAG actor may be able to halt or resume harts and force debug entry, gaining control over CPU execution and enabling subsequent debug memory/CSR access.

rtl_evidence.detail: jtag_key input exists; DMI request fields are assigned from shifted/address/data state and forwarded via dmi_cdc. The observed key handling only loads pass <= jtag_key on reset.
dmstatus.authenticated is hardwired to 1'b1; DMControl write assigns dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data; dmactive-low reset clears controls except writable dmactive.
dm_mem receives haltreq_i/resumereq_i and emits debug_req_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The full untruncated dmi_jtag state machine around pass_chk was not visible, but the observed forwarding path and unconditional dmstatus.authenticated are sufficient to support a candidate lack-of-auth finding.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: External JTAG/DMI operations can reach DMControl and hart halt/resume controls without a demonstrated authentication or lifecycle/debug-enable gate. dmstatus reports authenticated=1 unconditionally, and DMControl writes directly update the control register that feeds haltreq/resumereq.
inspection_records.notes: High-priority permission concern: decode/TAP access appears to substitute for authorization.

evidence_slots.subject: debug module system bus access master / DMI-JTAG requester
evidence_slots.operation: read/write system bus via debug module
evidence_slots.object: system memory and memory-mapped peripherals reachable through debug SBA/AXI
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug-originated system bus reads/writes should be constrained by debug authorization and any system address/PMP/range policy applicable to protected memory/peripherals; DMI CSR writes that launch SBA transfers should not bypass normal fabric permissions.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress/sbdata/SBCS writes from DMI. Writes to SBAddress0 and SBData0 assert sbaddress_write_valid_o or sbdata_write_valid_o when sberror is zero, while only setting sbbusyerror when busy. dm_top instantiates dm_sba with dmactive_i and connects it directly to axi_m_req_o/axi_m_resp_i. No address decode/range/firewall or authentication guard was observed in the provided evidence.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/DMI -> dm_csrs SBCS/SBAddress/SBData write cases -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> axi_m_req_o AXI master
evidence_slots.state_condition: SBA state is cleared when dmactive is low. Launch-related valid signals are blocked only by sberror and busy-error bookkeeping; dm_sba receives dmactive_i but no observed auth/range policy.
evidence_slots.impact: An external debug requester could read or overwrite arbitrary reachable system memory or MMIO, potentially bypassing CPU privilege, PMP/MMU policy, boot integrity, secrets isolation, or device control permissions.

rtl_evidence.detail: SBA controls are assigned from sbcs_q/sbaddr_q/sbdata_q; SBAddress0 and SBData0 writes set valid outputs with only sberror/busy handling.
dm_sba is connected to axi_m_req_o/axi_m_resp_i and to sbaddress/sbdata/control signals from dm_csrs.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The body of dm_sba and top-level fabric address map/firewall were not provided in full; exact reachable ranges are therefore uncertain.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug SBA DMI writes can launch AXI master reads/writes without an observed address range, requester, or authentication policy check.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is based on visible launch path and absence of observed range/auth mediation.

evidence_slots.subject: external debug requester
evidence_slots.object: global/non-debug reset and debug module active state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes that assert non-debug-module reset (ndmreset) or activate/deactivate the debug module should require authorized DMI access and should have safe reset defaults; reset control should not be reachable from unauthenticated/test paths.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: DMControl write case casts DMI data to dmcontrol and assigns dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data. When dmactive is low, the sequential block clears most fields including ndmreset but explicitly permits dmactive to be written. ndmreset_o is driven from dmcontrol state per module intent/ports, and dm_top exposes ndmreset_o to integration. No authorization check beyond valid DMI write was observed.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/DMI request -> dm_csrs DMControl write case -> dmcontrol_q.dmactive/ndmreset -> dmactive_o/ndmreset_o -> top-level reset/control integration
evidence_slots.state_condition: On rst_ni/dmactive-low, reset defaults clear control fields; dmactive remains writable during debug-module reset. dmi_rst_ni does not introduce authorization in the observed evidence.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated debug requester may reset non-debug logic or activate/deactivate the debug module, causing denial of service, state disruption, or preparation for further debug control.

rtl_evidence.detail: ndmreset_o/dmactive_o ports; DMControl write directly updates dmcontrol_d; dmactive is explicitly the only writable bit while inactive.
dm_top exposes ndmreset_o and dmactive_o at debug module top level.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact continuous assignments of ndmreset_o/dmactive_o were not shown in the excerpts, but port semantics and dmcontrol reset/update logic establish the control path.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI writes to DMControl can control dmactive and ndmreset without an observed authorization guard.
inspection_records.notes: Reset-safe defaults exist, but permission mediation for writes is not observed.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG reader
evidence_slots.object: DMI-readable debug CSR contents, system bus read data, abstract command data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Data returned over DMI/JTAG should be limited to authorized debug sessions and should not expose protected internal state, bus read data, or stale response data after reset/deactivation/error.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs accepts DMI reads when dmi_req_ready_o && dmi_req_valid_i && DTM_READ and drives resp_queue_data from data_q, dmstatus, and SBA-related registers. dmstatus.authenticated is hardwired true. dmi_cdc returns responses to JTAG; dmi_jtag_tap selects dmi_tdo_i for DMIACCESS and drives td_o/tdo_oe during shift. No authorization gating of read muxes or TDO path was observed.
evidence_slots.path: dm_csrs read mux/response queue -> dmi_cdc response path -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> td_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmi_jtag shift/request state resets on trst_ni; dmi_cdc uses trst_ni/rst_ni for CDC reset; dmactive-low clears debug CSR/SBA storage, but while active reads are gated by DMI valid/ready rather than authorization.
evidence_slots.impact: An external JTAG reader may extract debug state, abstract command data, or system bus read data, which can disclose privileged memory contents or sensitive execution state.

rtl_evidence.detail: DMI response valid/ready queue; authenticated hardwired; read path returns data_q and updates data/SBA data from internal/bus sources.
core DMI response is transferred to JTAG side.
DMIACCESS selects dmi_tdo_i for TDO; TDO output enable asserted during shift_ir or shift_dr.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full read case and response queue implementation were truncated; stale-data handling after all error states could not be completely assessed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI read responses expose debug CSR, abstract data, and SBA read data over JTAG without an observed authorization gate.
inspection_records.notes: Read exposure is tied to the same lack of debug authentication observed for halt/SBA.

evidence_slots.subject: software CSR interface / CPU privilege mode
evidence_slots.object: privileged CSRs and outputs including satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, debug_mode_o, single_step_o, cache enables, perf interface
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes and reads of privileged CSRs that control address translation, privilege status, trap vectors, debug mode, cache enables, or performance interfaces should be gated by current privilege/debug state; user-mode access should be denied or faulted.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: CSR access exception logic checks csr_addr privilege against priv_lvl_o, but priv_lvl_o is assigned to M when debug_mode_q or umode_i is true. Search results also show the access check exempts CSR_MEPC. Thus, if umode_i represents user mode as indicated by the obligation/fact layer, user-mode CSR operations are evaluated as machine privilege and can pass checks for privileged CSRs.
evidence_slots.path: csr_op_i/csr_addr_i/csr_wdata_i -> csr_we/csr_read -> CSR read/write cases -> access exception check using priv_lvl_o -> privileged CSR state/output updates
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset initializes priv_lvl_q to M and debug_mode_q to 0. Debug mode intentionally maps to M privilege; the same expression also maps umode_i to M.
evidence_slots.impact: User-mode software may read or modify privileged CSR state controlling address translation, trap handling, debug state, cache enables, and performance interface, enabling privilege escalation and isolation bypass.

rtl_evidence.detail: CSR interface and read/write cases; csr_we/csr_read generation; privilege exception check references priv_lvl_o; priv_lvl_o becomes M when debug_mode_q || umode_i; outputs expose translation/debug/control state.
MEPC read/write entries and explicit CSR_MEPC exception in privilege check per search observations.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact semantic source of umode_i was not provided beyond the fact layer naming it as user-mode condition; if umode_i is misnamed and actually means machine override, the finding would need revision.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: CSR privilege derivation appears to elevate when umode_i is asserted, undermining CSR privilege checks; MEPC is also explicitly exempted from the privilege check.
inspection_records.notes: This is a high-confidence candidate given the visible assignment and check relationship.

evidence_slots.subject: debug module and software CSR interface
evidence_slots.object: core debug mode state, debug PC redirection, single-step control, halt CSR behavior
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Entry to and control of debug mode should require a valid debug request source and should not be triggerable by ordinary CSR writes or unprivileged state transitions; debug-only CSRs should be accessible only while in debug mode.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_top connects DMI-controlled haltreq/resumereq to dm_mem, which emits debug_req_o. csr_regfile exposes debug_req_i and drives debug_mode_o/set_debug_pc_o/single_step_o. Reset clears debug_mode_q and dcsr_q; DRET clears debug_mode_q. However, upstream DMI authentication is absent/unconditional, and the CSR read process exposes DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH entries without an observed debug-mode-only condition in the read case excerpt.
evidence_slots.path: external JTAG/DMI -> dm_csrs DMControl/haltreq -> dm_top dm_mem debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i -> debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o/set_debug_pc_o/single_step_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: debug_mode_q resets to 0; DRET exits debug mode; interrupts are masked in debug mode; single_step_o is dcsr_q.step. Upstream dmactive controls debug module state but is itself DMI-writable.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized debug entry or debug CSR manipulation can stop normal execution, redirect PC into debug flow, enable single stepping, and expose privileged debug controls to software or external actors.

rtl_evidence.detail: dm_mem receives haltreq/resumereq and emits debug_req_o.
debug_req_i/debug outputs; DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH read entries; DRET exits debug; debug_mode and dcsr reset/output assignments.
debug DM reports authenticated true and accepts DMControl writes.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The exact csr_regfile debug_req_i transition code was not present in the visible excerpts, and full debug CSR write restrictions were truncated.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Core debug-mode entry is driven by debug_req_i and DMI-controlled halt/debug path without an observed upstream authorization guard; debug-only CSR read/write restrictions were not fully evidenced.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate relies on established unauthenticated upstream debug request path plus partially observed CSR debug controls.

evidence_slots.subject: test/DFT and JTAG interface

evidence_slots.object: DMI access selection, DMI reset, TDO output enable
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Testmode or DFT clock muxing should not enable DMI access, reset override, or TDO drive unless the same debug authorization and TAP state rules are satisfied; test-only controls should be isolated from protected debug operations in normal operation.

The excerpt does not show testmode_i directly selecting DMI access or bypassing the TAP instruction/state machine, but there is also no debug authorization gate on DMI/TDO in the observed path.

evidence_slots.state_condition: trst_ni resets td_o/tdo_oe_o; TAP FSM controls shift/capture/update; testmode_i affects clock mux at lines 219-224.
evidence_slots.impact: If testmode is externally controllable, it could affect TDO timing/drive and DMI accessibility; however, the provided evidence does not prove a direct testmode bypass of TAP or authorization.

rtl_evidence.detail: TDO mux selects DMI TDO for DMIACCESS; testmode_i selects clock mux; tdo_oe_o asserted during shift states; TAP FSM drives state controls.
dmi_jtag instantiates TAP and connects testmode_i, DMI access, reset, and TDO signals.

evidence_slots.impact: If general NoC traffic can target debug/reset/interrupt-control peripherals without requester or address permissions, remote tiles could reset/halt cores or manipulate timer/IPI/PLIC state; this impact is conditional on routing mediation details.

rtl_evidence.detail: Fact layer lists NoC channel ports for debug/bootrom/CLINT/PLIC, debug control outputs, and dmi_jtag/bootrom/apb_to_reg instances.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need serpent_peripherals internal assignments, wrappers, decode/routing conditions, and requester/channel permission checks.
inspection_records.notes: Attack surface is clear; permission mediation cannot be concluded from provided evidence.

19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES

33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
